name: Week 13 KB Conditioning — Heal Week, Full Variety (Spotlight: Bent Press) + Figure-8 + NEW Controlled-Power Flow
description: W13 is a shoulder-heal week; this KB day is **now on Saturday** (moved from Thursday — see the schedule change). It keeps **full variety, not trimmed** — KB flow work isn't the fatigue source. **Spotlight cycles back to bent press** per the rotation tracker — it rested W12, returns as a leverage-cue refresh. **The figure-8 → tactical clean flow stays** — a big W12D4 hit ('strength + cardio + very entertaining'). **NEW: a controlled-power flow block** (@metabolicfactor — around-body pass → side swing → high pull → goblet squat): flowy, passing-based (a cousin of the figure-8), non-overhead, the 'metabolism going' conditioning feel. **W12D5 lesson:** the figure-8 done 'more than written' tightened the QL — keep the dose honest. Legs day is now the day BEFORE this (Friday), so go easy on the loaded squatting in the flows. TGU + windmill are maintenance.

dayOverview.theme: Shoulder-heal week, KB day on Saturday now — full variety kept. Bent press cycles back to spotlight (leverage refresh). Figure-8 flow stays + a NEW controlled-power flow block adds fresh conditioning variety. Legs was yesterday — keep loaded squatting in the flows light. TGU + windmill maintenance.

spotlights.why: Cycles back to spotlight per the tracker (rested W12). W10 was its first spotlight — the leverage unlock: the body goes DOWN, the bell barely moves. W13 refreshes that cue at a light load. A deload Thursday is the ideal low-stakes week to re-groove a skill lift.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 windmill mechanics · W12 TGU left-side checkpoints · W13 bent press (current, DELOAD refresh) · W14 cycle forward — figure-8 flow stays in rotation, windmill/TGU rotate as needed.
watchpoints: **W13 is a shoulder-heal week — this KB day keeps FULL variety, now on Saturday.** The heal is in held loads, not a shortened session. Skill + flow + conditioning, no load chasing.

**Keep the dose honest on the flows.** W12D4 the user did the figure-8 'more than written' (to music) and the QL came in 'extremely tight' the next day. Legs was YESTERDAY (Friday) now — the QL is already worked, so go easy on the loaded squatting in the controlled-power + OTD flow blocks.
**TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift** — straight-arm overhead lockout, no rack hold during the get-up. Both sides feel fine now (W12D4), so this is maintenance, not rehab.
**Right shoulder watch on the figure-8 clean catch** — same crackly pattern as push days. If anything talks during the catch, drop to figure-8 passes only (no clean) and finish the round.
**Go easy on loaded squatting — legs was yesterday.** This Saturday KB day is a recovery flank for the legs; keep the goblet squats and flow squats light and controlled.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Slow circles, both directions. Shoulder loosener.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Mini-band overhead OR blue rope band gripped in hands.
Elbows drive to the ribs, scaps depress.
Press out to a T — fires rear delts and mid-traps.
Pull apart at the sternum, scaps retract, ribs down.
Sweep the arms back. Don't overarch.
15s each side. Primes the lateral hinge — directly preps the bent press.
Both arms in a V/Y overhead. Hold and breathe — lower traps fire.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension, no rest between positions.

sets.name: OTD Flow (Light Groove)

exercises.notes: Bell starts on the floor between the feet, hips drive the lift, catch in the rack with the knees slightly bent.
Goblet/rack position. Sit tall, full depth, no bouncing.
Stride out wide, sit into the working leg, keep the other leg long. Mobility, not strength.
Control on the way down — bell to the floor as cleanly as it came up.

sets.notes: **This is a continuous FLOW, not separate stations.** Move through the chain as one — dead clean → front squat → lateral lunge → tactical clean — letting each move feed the next, then switch sides and flow back. About 2 reps' worth at each move, but don't stop to count — let the rhythm carry it. 20lb throughout, 2 rounds. Deload + light groove — should feel effortless.

sets.name: 🔆 Skill Drilling (Bent Press Spotlight + TGU + Windmill)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD — the home-base skill weight; deload, no push). 2 rounds, ~4 reps each side.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — refresh the leverage cue (W10's unlock):**
- **The body goes DOWN; the bell barely moves.** This is the whole lift. It is NOT a press — you don't push the bell up, you lower your body underneath a bell that stays roughly where it is.
- **Bell locked overhead, eyes track the bell** the entire rep
- **Hinge and rotate AWAY from the bell side** — the same lateral-hinge family as the windmill
- **Reach the free hand down toward the inside of the front foot** as the torso lowers
- **Long spine throughout** — the bend is at the hip, not a crunch of the spine
- **Slow and deliberate** — bent press is a skill lift, fluidity over speed

**Reflection:** does the 'body down, bell still' feeling click this round, or are you still pressing the bell? If it still feels like a press, slow down and lighten — a deload Thursday is the perfect low-stakes week to re-find the leverage.

**LOAD:** 25lb (light — maintenance, both sides feel fine now per W12D4). 2 rounds.
- **Straight-arm overhead lockout** — punch the bell up, arm stays locked vertical the whole way; no rack hold during the get-up
- Eyes ON the bell every checkpoint
- Pause at every checkpoint — no rushing, the reverse is half the lift
- Hip bridge: glute squeeze, ribs down

**LOAD:** 18-20lb (light — maintenance after its W11 spotlight). 2 rounds.
- Bell locked overhead, eyes track the bell
- **Hip hinge AWAY from the bell side**
- **Stretch lives in the back leg's hip flexor, NOT the lower back**
- Slow descent, brief pause, controlled return

sets.name: Skill Block: Figure-8 → Tactical Clean (Stays in Rotation — PRESCRIBED Dose Only)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD: 12kg (26lb) only.** 2 rounds of 60s continuous flow.

**The figure-8-tactical-clean is ONE compound move** — the figure-8 pass exits into a clean to rack, repeated alternating sides:
- START in the hike position (bell between the legs, hinged at the hips)
- **Figure-8 → exit into a tactical clean to rack** — pass between the legs, hand catches BEHIND, the pull becomes the clean
- Lower the bell back through the hike position; the next pass switches sides
- Continue alternating for the full 60s

**Cues:**
- **The figure-8 EXITS into the clean** — the bell does not return to a hand mid-pass; the catch becomes the clean
- **Tactical clean = controlled, threaded into the rack** — no yank, no forearm slam
- **Hike is home base** — every transition resets through hike
- **Right shoulder watch on the rack catch** — if it talks, drop to figure-8 passes only and finish the round

**⚠️ DOSE DISCIPLINE — do the prescribed 2 rounds, NOT more.** W12D4 the user did this flow 'more than written' (to music) and W12D5 came in with the QL/psoas 'extremely tight.' The flow is fun — that's the trap. 2 rounds is the dose. Legs was yesterday — the QL is already worked, no need for bonus rounds.

sets.notes: Figure-8 flow stays in rotation (W12D4 confirmed it lands well). 2 rounds at 12kg, ~3 min work + rest. The discipline this week is doing the prescribed dose and stopping — the W12D5 QL tightness traced straight back to bonus rounds here.

sets.name: 🆕 Controlled-Power Flow (@metabolicfactor — NEW, Non-Overhead Conditioning)

exercises.notes: Pass the bell around the body, hand to hand — the passing-flow cousin of the figure-8. Stand tall, ribs down, let the bell travel smoothly.
One-arm swing out to the side on a diagonal — hips drive it, not the arm. Stay light.
Hinge-driven pull — elbow leads high, bell to upper chest. Sub-overhead, no pressing out at the top.
Bell at the chest, full depth, sit tall. **Legs were yesterday — keep these light and controlled.**

sets.notes: **🆕 NEW — @metabolicfactor controlled-power circuit, added W13 for fresh variety.** Around-body pass → side swing → high pull → goblet squat, flowing continuously, 2 rounds. **Non-overhead and passing-based** — the flowy 'metabolism going' feel you liked from the figure-8, with no overhead pressing to tax the healing shoulder. Single light bell (~20-26lb). Keep the goblet squats light — legs were yesterday.

exercises.notes: 60s each side. Hip flexor release — counters the TGU hip bridge + lateral lunge work.
60s. Adductors get tight from the lateral lunges + figure-8 stance.

tips: **W13 is a shoulder-heal week — this KB day keeps full variety, now on Saturday.** Skill + flow + conditioning; the heal is in held loads, not a shortened session.
**Spotlight: bent press** — refresh the leverage cue (body goes DOWN, bell barely moves). A heal week is a low-stakes window to re-groove a skill lift.
**Figure-8 flow stays — keep the dose honest.** W12D4's bonus rounds tightened the QL the next day. 2 rounds is the dose; fun is the trap.
**🆕 NEW: controlled-power flow** (@metabolicfactor — around-body pass → side swing → high pull → goblet squat). Non-overhead, passing-based — fresh variety in the figure-8 family.
**TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift** — straight-arm overhead lockout, no rack hold during the get-up. Maintenance this week — both sides feel fine.
**Right shoulder watch on the figure-8 rack catch** — drop to passes only if it talks.
**Legs was yesterday** — go easy on loaded squatting in the flows; this Saturday KB day is a recovery flank for the legs.
Rotation plan: W14 cycles forward — figure-8 flow stays, windmill/TGU rotate as needed.
formPhilosophy: Shoulder-heal week, KB day on Saturday — full variety kept, not a shrunken session. Skill + flow + conditioning: bent press leverage, the figure-8 flow, the new controlled-power circuit. The heal is in held loads and managed overhead volume. The discipline is dosing the flows honestly — a fun flow is the easiest thing to overdo.
